summary: Frontend-only retest completed for sorting pseudocode mapping. Quick Sort semantic mapping is fixed for pivot/compare/swap steps, and no out-of-range highlighting was seen for most algorithms. One HIGH issue remains in Selection Sort swap highlighting.

ui_bugs.issue: During active swap operations, no pseudocode line is highlighted (active_line=null).

integration_issues.issue: Swap step emits line=6 while Selection Sort pseudocode has only 5 lines, causing out-of-range mapping and empty highlight.

action_items: Fix Selection Sort swap step mapping in sortingAlgorithms.js from line 6 to line 5 (or align pseudocode accordingly).
Add regression test that asserts active pseudocode line exists for every emitted step that has line > 0.
Re-run sorting retest after selection mapping fix.
critical_code_review_comments: selectionSort records swap with line: 6, but selection pseudocode has 5 entries, creating invalid highlight index.
Quick Sort mapping appears corrected: pivot/compare/swap now align to lines 1/2 in UI runtime checks.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Primary retest scope was pseudocode mapping. Broad algorithm scan passed for bubble/insertion/merge/quick/heap/shell/counting, but targeted reproduction confirmed Selection Sort swap steps show no active pseudocode line. Repro is stable with Selection Sort, array size ~25, speed ~35.
rca of the issue: RCA: Selection Sort swap step metadata uses out-of-range line value. In /app/frontend/src/modules/sorting/sortingAlgorithms.js, selectionSort swap record sets line: 6 while Selection Sort pseudocode array contains only 5 lines. Repro: open /sorting, select Selection Sort, start sort; when title shows 'Swap index X with min index Y', no pseudocode row is highlighted (active_line null). Mitigation: update step line mapping to 5 or expand pseudocode lines consistently with emitted step indices.
